enrichment.short_description: ReviewTrackers is a Chicago-based online reputation and review management platform that helps multi-location brands monitor, generate, and respond to customer reviews across 100+ review sites.
enrichment.pricing_model: Per-location subscription tiers (Data Only, Starter, Essential) plus custom-quoted add-ons; most published prices require a custom quote from sales.
enrichment.starting_price: Plans reported to start around $49 to $89 per location per month (billed annually); exact pricing requires a custom quote
enrichment.seo_title: ReviewTrackers Review 2026: Pricing, Features and Alternatives
enrichment.seo_meta_description: See ReviewTrackers pricing, features, and pros and cons for 2026. Compare this Chicago-based review management and reputation platform before you buy.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: ReviewTrackers is an online reputation and review management platform founded in 2012 and headquartered in Chicago, Illinois. The company, which has around 51-200 employees and was later acquired by customer experience firm InMoment, builds software that helps multi-location businesses such as retail chains, healthcare groups, auto dealers, and franchises monitor, generate, and respond to customer reviews. The core product aggregates review data from more than 100 sources, including Google, Facebook, and Yelp, into a single dashboard so brand and marketing teams can track sentiment and volume across every location from one place. Beyond monitoring, ReviewTrackers offers automated review generation through SMS and email requests sent after a customer interaction, AI-assisted review response suggestions to help teams reply faster and more consistently, and keyword and sentiment alerts that flag reviews mentioning specific terms in real time. Higher tiers add competitor benchmarking and experience analysis, letting brands compare their review volume and sentiment against named competitors, plus embeddable review widgets for websites. ReviewTrackers sells its platform through three main plans: Data Only, which provides raw aggregated review data for use in a company's own systems or BI tools; Starter, which covers the basics with unlimited users, dashboards, review solicitation, AI response assistance, and alerting; and Essential, which layers on competitor insights and a review carousel widget. Optional add-on modules include local listings management, local search rank tracking, managed services, white-label reporting, an employer brand package, and an app store review package, all sold on custom quotes. Pricing is charged per business location rather than per user, and published third-party sources report starting prices anywhere from roughly $49 to $89 per location per month when billed annually, though ReviewTrackers does not publish exact enterprise or multi-location numbers on its site and generally requires prospective customers to request a custom quote. A free trial is available for prospective customers to evaluate the platform. ReviewTrackers positions itself around the statistic that the large majority of consumers use online reviews to guide everyday purchasing decisions, and its target customers are typically multi-location or franchise brands that need centralized reputation oversight across dozens or hundreds of storefronts rather than a single-location business.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: ReviewTrackers was founded in 2012 and is headquartered in Chicago, Illinois. The company builds reputation management software for multi-location businesses, aggregating reviews from more than 100 sources into a single platform. It was later acquired by customer experience company InMoment, and today serves industries such as retail, healthcare, automotive, and franchise brands that need to monitor and respond to reviews across many locations at once.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: The platform combines review monitoring, automated SMS and email review generation, AI-assisted review responses, and keyword or sentiment alerts in a single dashboard. Higher-tier customers also get competitor benchmarking, experience analysis, and embeddable review widgets, while add-on modules extend the platform into local listings management, local search rank tracking, and white-label reporting for agencies and enterprise brands.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: ReviewTrackers prices per business location across three tiers: Data Only, Starter, and Essential, with unlimited users included on the platform plans. Third-party pricing trackers report starting prices in the roughly $49 to $89 per location per month range when billed annually, but exact figures for most tiers, and all add-on modules, require a custom quote directly from ReviewTrackers.

faqs.answer: Yes, ReviewTrackers offers a free trial so prospective customers can evaluate the platform before subscribing.

faqs.answer: It aggregates reviews from over 100 sources, including major platforms like Google, Facebook, and Yelp, plus industry-specific review sites.

faqs.answer: ReviewTrackers was acquired by customer experience management company InMoment.

faqs.answer: Pricing is based on business location, with unlimited users included on the platform tiers.

faqs.answer: Essential includes everything in Starter plus competitor insights, experience analysis, and a carousel review widget.

faqs.answer: Yes, the platform supports automated review solicitation via both SMS and email.

enrichment.short_description: Revive Adserver is a free, open-source ad-serving platform descended from OpenX Source and phpAdsNew that lets publishers and ad networks manage and deliver banner, text, and video ads from a self-hosted server.
enrichment.pricing_model: Free and open source (GPL license) for self-hosted use; an optional hosted edition is available from third-party providers for a monthly fee.
enrichment.company_size: Open-source community project (no fixed employee count); currently maintained by Aqua Platform
enrichment.headquarters: No corporate headquarters - open-source project; originally developed under OpenX Technologies, now maintained by Aqua Platform
languages: plus community-contributed translations into multiple other languages
enrichment.seo_title: Revive Adserver Review 2026: Free Open-Source Ad Server Guide
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Explore Revive Adserver, the free open-source ad server descended from OpenX. See features, self-hosting requirements, and hosted pricing for 2026.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Revive Adserver is a free, open-source ad-serving platform licensed under the GNU General Public License, used by publishers, ad networks, and agencies to manage and deliver banner, text, rich media, and video ad campaigns from a self-hosted server. Its lineage traces back to the phpAdsNew project led by Wim Godden starting around 2000 to 2001, which evolved into Openads and then, in 2007, into OpenX Source. In September 2013, the OpenX Source codebase was acquired and rebranded as Revive Adserver, with its first release under the new name being version 3.0.0. Intellectual property rights passed to Revive Software and Services in 2015, and stewardship of the project was transferred again in 2024 to Aqua Platform, which now maintains the software and offers an optional hosted edition. As of mid-2026 the current stable self-hosted release is version 6.0.8. Because it is a community open-source project rather than a conventional company, Revive Adserver has no single corporate headquarters or fixed employee count; development is driven by a mix of paid maintainers and community contributors listed on its GitHub repository, and the project accepts donations and sponsorships through Patreon from businesses that generate revenue using the software. Functionally, Revive Adserver provides zone-based ad targeting by browser, domain, and language; standard banner rotation and click and impression tracking; frequency/ad capping to limit how often a visitor sees the same creative; and, since the introduction of a plugin architecture in version 2.8 of the OpenX-era codebase, an extensible system for adding custom functionality. It also offers a REST API for programmatic campaign and inventory management. The self-hosted, downloadable edition is completely free to use on a publisher's or agency's own infrastructure, with no licensing or per-impression fees, making it a popular choice for independent publishers and smaller ad networks that want to avoid vendor lock-in and retain full control of their ad-serving data. For organizations that do not want to manage their own servers, Aqua Platform offers a hosted edition of Revive Adserver starting at approximately $10 per month, which removes the need for self-managed infrastructure while still running the same open-source codebase. Because it is open source, Revive Adserver does not have traditional SaaS-style pricing tiers, customer support contracts, or a sales organization in the way commercial ad servers do; support instead comes primarily from community forums, documentation, and GitHub, with the hosted edition offering more direct technical assistance.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Revive Adserver is an open-source ad server whose codebase traces back to the phpAdsNew project from around 2000-2001, which became Openads and then OpenX Source by 2007. When OpenX Source was acquired and rebranded in September 2013, it became Revive Adserver, released under the GNU General Public License. Project stewardship passed to Revive Software and Services in 2015 and then to Aqua Platform in 2024, which maintains the software today and offers a paid hosted edition alongside the free self-hosted download.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: The platform supports zone-based ad targeting, banner rotation, click and impression tracking, frequency capping, and multiple ad formats including banner, text, rich media, and video. A plugin architecture, introduced in the OpenX era, allows developers to extend core functionality, and a REST API enables programmatic management of campaigns and inventory.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: The downloadable, self-hosted edition of Revive Adserver is completely free under its GPL open-source license, with no licensing or per-impression fees. Organizations that prefer not to manage their own servers can use a hosted edition offered by maintainer Aqua Platform, which starts at approximately $10 per month. The project also accepts voluntary sponsorships via Patreon from businesses earning revenue through the software.

faqs.question: Is Revive Adserver really free?
faqs.answer: Yes, the self-hosted, downloadable edition is free and open source under the GPL license, with no licensing fees.

faqs.question: What license does Revive Adserver use?
faqs.answer: It is released under the GNU General Public License (GPL).

faqs.question: Who maintains Revive Adserver today?
faqs.answer: The project is currently maintained by Aqua Platform, which took over stewardship in 2024.

faqs.question: What was Revive Adserver called before?
faqs.answer: It was formerly known as OpenX Source, and before that Openads and phpAdsNew.

faqs.question: Does Revive Adserver support video ads?
faqs.answer: Yes, in addition to banner and text ads, it supports rich media and video ad formats.

faqs.question: Can I get a hosted version of Revive Adserver?
faqs.answer: Yes, Aqua Platform offers a hosted edition starting at around $10 per month for users who do not want to self-host.

faqs.question: What are the server requirements for self-hosting?
faqs.answer: Revive Adserver runs on a standard PHP and MySQL server environment that publishers manage themselves.

faqs.question: Does Revive Adserver have an API?
faqs.answer: Yes, it offers a REST API for programmatic campaign and inventory management.

enrichment.short_description: Revolut Business is the business banking and spend-management arm of London-based fintech Revolut, offering multi-currency accounts, corporate cards, and payment tools for companies of all sizes.
enrichment.pricing_model: Tiered monthly subscription (Basic, Grow, Scale) plus custom Enterprise pricing; annual billing offers a discount over monthly billing.
languages: and multiple other languages supported across Revolut's global app
enrichment.seo_title: Revolut Business Pricing and Features 2026: Full Review
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Compare Revolut Business pricing plans, multi-currency features, and corporate card benefits for 2026 in this detailed review.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Revolut Business is the business banking and spend-management product line of Revolut, the London-headquartered fintech company founded in July 2015 by Nik Storonskiy and Vlad Yatsenko. While the wider Revolut group has grown into a large financial technology company with tens of thousands of employees globally and a reported valuation of around $75 billion as of a November 2025 share sale, and serves more than 70 million customers worldwide, Revolut Business specifically targets companies ranging from small startups to larger enterprises that need multi-currency accounts and modern payment tools without relying solely on a traditional bank. In the United States, Revolut Business is sold across four tiers: Basic, which is free and includes a multi-currency account plus a small number of free transfers per month; Grow, priced at $30 per month, which adds 100 free local transfers, 5 free international payments, up to $10,000 in fee-free FX exchange, bulk payments, and a complimentary metal card; Scale, priced at $119 per month, which raises those allowances to 1,000 free local transfers, 25 free international payments, and $80,000 in fee-free exchange while adding API access and granular team permissions; and Enterprise, which is custom-priced and includes tailored allowances, a dedicated account manager, and priority support with defined response times. Annual billing can save up to 22% compared with paying monthly. Core features across the product include the ability to hold, send, and receive 25 or more currencies at or near the interbank exchange rate, physical and virtual corporate cards with real-time spend controls, bulk payment tools capable of paying up to 1,000 suppliers or staff members at once, and accounting integrations with platforms such as Xero and QuickBooks. Higher tiers unlock API access for custom integrations and more granular role-based permissions for finance teams. Because Revolut operates primarily as an e-money institution rather than a full bank in many markets, deposit protections and regulatory treatment can differ from a traditional bank account depending on the customer's country, and feature availability, plan names, and pricing vary somewhat by region. Revolut Business is most commonly used by e-commerce companies paying international suppliers, startups that need multi-currency accounts from day one, and finance teams that want bulk payroll or vendor payment tools without juggling multiple banking relationships.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Revolut was founded in July 2015 by Nik Storonskiy and Vlad Yatsenko and is headquartered in London, United Kingdom. Revolut Business is the company's dedicated banking and spend-management product for companies, built on top of the same multi-currency infrastructure that powers Revolut's consumer app. The wider Revolut group has grown into one of the largest fintechs globally, with a valuation of around $75 billion reported in late 2025 and tens of thousands of employees worldwide.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Revolut Business lets companies hold, send, and receive 25-plus currencies at competitive exchange rates, issue physical and virtual corporate cards with real-time spend controls, and make bulk payments to up to 1,000 payees at once. Higher-tier plans add API access for custom integrations and granular, role-based team permissions for finance teams, along with accounting software integrations.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: In the US, Revolut Business is sold across four plans: Basic (free), Grow ($30/month), Scale ($119/month), and a custom-priced Enterprise tier. Each paid tier increases the number of free local and international transfers and the amount of fee-free currency exchange included, and annual billing offers savings of up to 22% versus paying monthly.

faqs.question: Is Revolut Business a bank?
faqs.answer: In many markets Revolut operates under an e-money license rather than as a full bank, though this varies by region and has been changing as Revolut obtains banking licenses in more countries.

faqs.answer: Yes, the Basic plan is free and includes a multi-currency account with a limited number of free transfers.

faqs.question: How does pricing compare across Revolut Business tiers?
faqs.answer: In the US, plans run Basic (free), Grow ($30/month), Scale ($119/month), and custom-priced Enterprise, with increasing transfer allowances and features at each tier.

faqs.question: Does Revolut Business offer an API?
faqs.answer: Yes, API access is included on the Scale and Enterprise plans for building custom integrations.

faqs.question: What is the difference between Grow and Scale?
faqs.answer: Scale offers significantly higher free transfer and FX allowances plus API access and more granular team permissions than Grow.

faqs.question: What accounting software does Revolut Business integrate with?
faqs.answer: It integrates with accounting platforms such as Xero and QuickBooks, among others.

faqs.answer: Yes, annual subscriptions can save up to 22% compared to monthly billing.

enrichment.short_description: Rho is a New York-based fintech offering an all-in-one business banking, corporate card, and treasury management platform for startups and scale-ups, with no subscription or per-seat fees.
enrichment.pricing_model: Free business banking and corporate cards with no subscription or per-user fees; Rho Treasury charges an assets-under-management-based fee.
enrichment.starting_price: Free (no monthly subscription fees for core banking and cards)
enrichment.headquarters: New York, New York, United States (100 Crosby Street)
enrichment.seo_title: Rho Review 2026: Business Banking and Spend Management Pricing
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Read our 2026 review of Rho's business banking platform, covering corporate cards, treasury management, fees, and features for startups.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Rho is a fintech company headquartered at 100 Crosby Street in New York City, founded in 2018 by Everett Cook, Alex Wheldon, and Damian Kimmelman. The company builds an all-in-one financial operating system for businesses, combining business checking accounts, corporate credit cards, accounts payable automation, expense management, and treasury management on a single platform. Rho has raised a total of roughly $235 million in funding across multiple rounds, including a $75 million Series B in 2021 led by Dragoneer Capital and DFJ Growth with participation from M13 Ventures, Inspired Capital, and Torch Capital, followed by a later Series C round in 2024; investors also include Balderton Capital, Leaders Fund, and Community Investment Management. As of 2026 the company employs roughly 334 people. Rho's central pitch is finance automation without the subscription fees typical of many competing platforms: there are no monthly subscription costs, no minimum balance fees on checking accounts, no per-user or per-card fees, and no charges for same-day ACH transfers, wire transfers, or checks. Its corporate cards carry no annual fee and earn cash back of up to 1.5% on spend, and the platform bundles accounts payable automation and expense management with customizable approval workflows so finance teams can manage bill pay, employee spend, and vendor payments from one place. Where Rho does charge a fee is through Rho Treasury, which operates as an SEC-registered investment advisor; Rho Treasury charges a tiered annual management fee ranging from about 0.15% for deposits of $20 million or more up to about 0.6% for deposits under $2 million, in exchange for access to higher-yield cash management and a sweep network that extends FDIC insurance coverage on deposits up to $75 million. Rho positions itself primarily for venture-backed startups and scale-up companies in the United States that need banking, card, and treasury tools without paying separate subscription fees for each function, and it emphasizes 24/7 customer support as part of its core offering. Because Rho is not itself a chartered bank, its banking services are provided in partnership with FDIC-insured partner banks, a common structure among modern fintech banking platforms.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Rho was founded in 2018 by Everett Cook, Alex Wheldon, and Damian Kimmelman and is headquartered in New York City. The company has raised roughly $235 million in funding, including a $75 million Series B round in 2021, and employs around 334 people as of 2026. Rho builds a combined banking, corporate card, and treasury management platform aimed at startups and scale-up companies.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Rho offers no-fee business checking accounts, corporate cards with up to 1.5% cash back and no annual fees, accounts payable automation, and expense management with customizable approval workflows. Rho Treasury adds access to higher-yield cash management and a sweep network offering FDIC insurance coverage up to $75 million, operated under Rho's status as an SEC-registered investment advisor.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: Rho does not charge monthly subscription fees, per-user fees, minimum balance fees, or fees for same-day ACH, wires, or checks on its core banking and card products. The exception is Rho Treasury, which charges an annual, tiered management fee ranging from about 0.15% for larger balances of $20 million or more up to about 0.6% for balances under $2 million.

faqs.question: Is Rho a bank?
faqs.answer: Rho is a fintech company; its banking services, such as FDIC insurance, are provided through partner banks rather than Rho itself holding a bank charter.

faqs.answer: No, Rho does not charge monthly subscription fees, per-user fees, or minimum balance fees on its core banking and card products.

faqs.question: How much cash back do Rho corporate cards offer?

faqs.question: What is Rho Treasury?
faqs.answer: Rho Treasury is an SEC-registered investment advisory service that offers higher-yield cash management, charging a tiered annual management fee based on deposit size.

faqs.question: How much FDIC insurance does Rho provide?
faqs.answer: Through its sweep network via Rho Treasury, Rho can extend FDIC insurance coverage on deposits up to $75 million.

faqs.question: Who founded Rho?
faqs.answer: Rho was founded in 2018 by Everett Cook, Alex Wheldon, and Damian Kimmelman.

faqs.answer: Rho has raised approximately $235 million across multiple funding rounds, including a $75 million Series B in 2021.

faqs.question: Does Rho support international payments?
faqs.answer: Rho charges a 1% fee on foreign currency transfers as part of its cross-border payment capabilities.

Access to higher-yield cash management and money market options

enrichment.short_description: RhodeCode is a Berlin-based enterprise source code management platform that unifies Git, Mercurial, and Subversion repositories with code review, permissions, and integrations, available as a free open-source Community Edition or paid Enterprise and Cloud editions.
enrichment.pricing_model: Free open-source Community Edition; paid Enterprise Edition licensed per user per year; paid Cloud edition billed per user per month, each with a 10-user minimum.
enrichment.starting_price: Free (Community Edition); Enterprise from $75/user/year; Cloud from $8/user/month
enrichment.company_size: Small, privately held company (exact headcount not publicly disclosed)
enrichment.headquarters: Berlin, Germany (with an additional office in Palo Alto, California)
enrichment.seo_title: RhodeCode Review 2026: Pricing and Features for Git, SVN, Mercurial
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Compare RhodeCode Community, Enterprise, and Cloud pricing and features for managing Git, Mercurial, and Subversion repositories in 2026.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: RhodeCode is an enterprise source code management platform originally started in 2010 by Marcin Kuzminski, with the company formally incorporated in July 2013 alongside co-founder Sebastian Kreutzberger. The company is headquartered in Berlin, Germany, and also maintains an office in Palo Alto, California; it is a small, privately held company that has not publicly disclosed a precise employee count. RhodeCode's core product distinguishes itself from single-VCS platforms like GitHub or GitLab by unifying three version control systems, Git, Mercurial, and Subversion, under one centralized management layer, letting organizations apply common authentication, permissions, and code review workflows regardless of which VCS a given team or repository uses. This makes it particularly relevant to larger enterprises, regulated industries, and organizations with legacy Subversion or Mercurial repositories that still need centralized governance alongside newer Git-based projects. RhodeCode is offered in three editions. RhodeCode Community Edition (CE) is free and open source, supports an unlimited number of users and repositories, and can be deployed entirely on-premises, including fine-grained permission controls, code review tools, full-text code search, APIs for custom tool integration, and basic LDAP/Active Directory authentication, backed by community support. RhodeCode Enterprise Edition (EE) is the paid, for-fee tier built on top of CE, priced at $75 per user per year with a minimum purchase of 10 users (sold in 10-user packs, with volume and multi-year discounts available); it adds features such as technical support with a service-level agreement, built-in code chat and smart reviewer assignment for instant code reviews, scalable code search across all repositories, high-availability multi-node deployment architecture, predefined integrations with tools like JIRA, Jenkins, Bamboo, and Slack, and unified enterprise authentication covering LDAP with groups, SAML, Active Directory, CAS, Google, OAuth, and two-factor authentication. A newer RhodeCode Cloud offering starts at $8 per user per month, also with a 10-user minimum, and includes all Enterprise Edition features on managed infrastructure with a dedicated domain and enterprise-grade SSL, unlimited traffic, disk space, and repository size, dedicated isolated hardware, geo-located instances across the US, Asia, and Europe, daily backups, and built-in error and performance monitoring, billed as a cancel-anytime monthly subscription. RhodeCode targets software developers, project managers, and DevOps engineers at organizations that need centralized, compliant control over multiple version control systems rather than a single-VCS developer platform.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: RhodeCode originated in 2010 under founder Marcin Kuzminski and was formally incorporated in 2013 with co-founder Sebastian Kreutzberger. The company is headquartered in Berlin, Germany, with an additional office in Palo Alto, California, and remains a small, privately held business. Its product targets software developers, project managers, and DevOps engineers who need centralized control over Git, Mercurial, and Subversion repositories within a single organization.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: RhodeCode's standout capability is unifying three version control systems under one authentication, permission, and code review layer, rather than supporting only Git. Enterprise customers get instant code reviews with built-in chat, scalable cross-repository search, high-availability multi-node deployment, and integrations with tools like JIRA, Jenkins, Bamboo, and Slack, along with enterprise authentication options including SAML, LDAP, CAS, OAuth, and two-factor authentication.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: RhodeCode Community Edition is free and open source with unlimited users and repositories for self-hosted, on-premises deployment. RhodeCode Enterprise Edition costs $75 per user per year with a 10-user minimum, while the newer RhodeCode Cloud offering starts at $8 per user per month, also with a 10-user minimum, providing managed hosting with all Enterprise features included.

faqs.answer: RhodeCode supports Git, Mercurial, and Subversion repositories within a single unified platform.

faqs.question: What's the difference between RhodeCode Enterprise and Cloud editions?
faqs.answer: Enterprise is a self-hosted, per-user-per-year license at $75/user/year, while Cloud is a managed hosting subscription starting at $8/user/month with all Enterprise features included.

faqs.question: Is there a minimum number of users for paid plans?
faqs.answer: Yes, both Enterprise Edition and Cloud require a minimum of 10 users.

faqs.question: Who founded RhodeCode?

faqs.answer: Yes, both the Community and Enterprise editions can be deployed entirely on-premises.

faqs.answer: Yes, two-factor authentication is available as part of RhodeCode Enterprise's unified authentication options.

enrichment.short_description: Richpanel is a San Jose-based AI-powered customer service and helpdesk platform built specifically for e-commerce brands, unifying email, chat, social, and self-service support in one inbox.
enrichment.pricing_model: Per-agent monthly subscription billed annually (Pro, Pro Max), custom Enterprise pricing, plus an optional AI agent add-on and a separate self-service portal add-on.
enrichment.company_size: Small-to-midsize private company (exact headcount not publicly disclosed)
enrichment.seo_title: Richpanel Review 2026: Pricing and Features for Ecommerce Support
enrichment.seo_meta_description: See Richpanel pricing plans, AI agent add-on costs, and helpdesk features for 2026 e-commerce customer support teams.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Richpanel is a customer service software company founded in 2019 and headquartered in San Jose, California, that builds AI-powered helpdesk infrastructure specifically for e-commerce and direct-to-consumer brands rather than general-purpose businesses. The platform combines three layers in one product: multi-channel support infrastructure that centralizes email, live chat, and social media conversations into a single agent inbox; AI-powered automation, including an AI customer service agent that can resolve tickets on its own; and self-service components that let shoppers manage their own orders, returns, and exchanges without needing to contact an agent. On its help desk side, Richpanel sells two named self-serve plans plus a custom Enterprise tier: Pro, at $59 per agent per month when billed annually (equivalent to $708 per year), covers up to three seats and includes email, live chat, and social channel support along with basic reporting, automation rules, and AI features included at no extra cost; Pro Max, at $89 per agent per month billed annually ($1,068 per year), adds SMS and phone support, unlimited automations and campaigns, support for multiple brands and languages, and more advanced reporting covering revenue impact and customer satisfaction; and Enterprise, which requires a minimum of 10 agents, is custom priced, and includes white-glove onboarding, a campaign manager, and custom integrations. Separately, Richpanel also sells a standalone AI agent product, where a single AI agent costs $500 per month plus an additional $100 per seat for the helpdesk the human team works in, reflecting a per-store rather than strictly per-agent pricing philosophy for its AI offering. A self-service portal add-on, covering order management, returns processing, a branded help center, and custom automation flows, is available separately for roughly $99 to $500 per month depending on scale. All plans include 24/5 customer support, white-glove migration assistance from other helpdesk tools, the ability to remove Richpanel branding, and a contract buyout program for customers switching from competitors; Richpanel also advertises a guarantee of at least 30% savings versus platforms like Zendesk or Gorgias along with a 30% reduction in ticket volume within 60 days, or a refund. Richpanel integrates natively with Shopify and other e-commerce platforms, making it best suited to online retailers that want AI-driven automation and self-service tightly coupled with their storefront rather than a horizontal, industry-agnostic support tool.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Richpanel was founded in 2019 and is headquartered in San Jose, California. The company builds AI-powered customer service software built exclusively for e-commerce and direct-to-consumer brands, combining a unified support inbox, AI automation, and customer self-service tools into a single platform aimed at reducing support ticket volume for online retailers.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Richpanel centralizes email, live chat, social media, SMS, and phone channels into one inbox, layers in AI-driven automation and an optional AI support agent, and gives customers a self-service portal to manage orders and returns without contacting an agent. Higher tiers add multi-brand and multi-language support along with revenue-impact and CSAT reporting.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: Richpanel's help desk is sold as Pro at $59/agent/month (billed annually), Pro Max at $89/agent/month (billed annually), and a custom-priced Enterprise tier requiring at least 10 agents. A standalone AI agent add-on costs $500/month plus $100 per seat, and a self-service portal add-on runs roughly $99 to $500/month depending on scale.

enrichment.short_description: RingCentral is a publicly traded, Belmont, California-based provider of AI-powered cloud business communications, offering phone, messaging, video meetings, and contact center software under its RingEX (formerly RingCentral MVP) platform.
enrichment.pricing_model: Per-user monthly subscription tiers (Core, Advanced, Ultra), with lower per-user rates for annual billing; Contact Center is sold as a separate add-on.

languages: and multiple other languages supported for its global customer base
enrichment.seo_title: RingCentral Pricing 2026: Plans, Features and Full Review
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Compare RingCentral RingEX pricing tiers, features, and pros and cons for 2026 cloud business phone and communications.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: RingCentral, Inc. is a publicly traded (NYSE: RNG) provider of cloud-based business communications founded in 1999 and headquartered in Belmont, California. The company reports trailing twelve-month revenue of roughly $2.5 billion and employee counts that vary by source from around 4,260 to over 7,000, reflecting its growth from an early VoIP provider into one of the larger unified-communications-as-a-service (UCaaS) vendors globally. Its flagship product, historically branded RingCentral MVP (Message, Video, Phone) and now marketed as RingEX, bundles a cloud-hosted business phone system, team messaging, video meetings, and SMS/MMS texting into a single per-user subscription, with a separate Contact Center product available as an add-on for customer service and call center use cases. RingEX is sold in three tiers. The Core plan starts at $20 per user per month when billed annually (or $30 per user per month billed monthly) and includes unlimited calling within the US and Canada, voicemail-to-text, call queues, an auto-attendant for automated call routing, video meetings for up to 100 participants, and 25 SMS messages per user per month, though it lacks automatic call recording. The Advanced plan runs $25 per user per month annually (or $35 monthly) and adds features such as automatic call recording and higher usage limits on top of everything in Core. The Ultra plan, at $35 per user per month annually (or $45 monthly), is the top tier and includes everything in Advanced plus expanded video meeting capacity of up to 200 participants, unlimited storage, and device analytics. Across all tiers, RingCentral emphasizes AI-powered features branded as RingCentral AI, including call summaries, transcription, and assistant tools, and the platform integrates with more than 300 third-party applications including Microsoft 365, Google Workspace, and Salesforce. Because RingCentral's list pricing does not always include compliance fees, additional phone numbers, or usage overages, actual invoiced costs commonly run 20-35% above the advertised per-user rate according to several third-party pricing analyses. RingCentral is used across organizations of all sizes, from small businesses replacing a legacy phone system to large enterprises consolidating multiple communication vendors into a single UCaaS platform, and its Contact Center add-on extends the core phone and messaging platform into full omnichannel customer service deployments for larger call center operations.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: RingCentral, Inc. was founded in 1999 and is headquartered in Belmont, California. It is a publicly traded company on the NYSE under the ticker RNG, with trailing twelve-month revenue of roughly $2.5 billion and an employee base reported between roughly 4,260 and over 7,000 people depending on the source. The company has grown into one of the major providers of cloud-based unified communications for businesses of all sizes.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: RingCentral's core RingEX platform (formerly branded RingCentral MVP) combines a cloud business phone system, team messaging, video meetings, and SMS texting in one subscription. It includes call queues, an auto-attendant, and AI-powered features such as call summaries and transcription, plus integrations with over 300 third-party apps including Microsoft 365, Google Workspace, and Salesforce. A separate Contact Center product extends the platform for customer service teams.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: RingEX is priced per user per month across three tiers: Core, Advanced, and Ultra. Annual billing brings the price down to $20, $25, and $35 per user per month respectively, versus $30, $35, and $45 per user per month for monthly billing. Contact Center is sold separately as an add-on, and actual invoices often exceed list pricing once fees, extra numbers, and usage overages are included.

faqs.question: What is RingCentral RingEX?
faqs.answer: RingEX is RingCentral's core unified communications platform, formerly branded RingCentral MVP, combining phone, video, and messaging.

faqs.question: How much does RingCentral cost per user?
faqs.answer: Pricing ranges from about $20 to $35 per user per month with annual billing, or $30 to $45 per user per month billed monthly, across the Core, Advanced, and Ultra tiers.

faqs.question: What's the difference between Core, Advanced, and Ultra?
faqs.answer: Each tier adds features and higher usage limits, with Advanced adding automatic call recording and Ultra adding larger video meetings, unlimited storage, and device analytics.

faqs.question: Does RingCentral include video meetings?
faqs.answer: Yes, video meetings for up to 100 participants are included on Core, rising to 200 participants on the Ultra tier.

faqs.question: Is there a contact center option?
faqs.answer: Yes, RingCentral Contact Center is available as a separate add-on to any RingEX plan.

faqs.question: How many participants can join a RingCentral video meeting?
faqs.answer: Depending on the plan, RingCentral video meetings support up to 100 or up to 200 participants.

faqs.question: Is RingCentral publicly traded?
faqs.answer: Yes, RingCentral, Inc. trades on the NYSE under the ticker symbol RNG.

faqs.question: What integrations does RingCentral support?
faqs.answer: RingCentral integrates with more than 300 third-party applications, including Microsoft 365, Google Workspace, and Salesforce.

enrichment.short_description: RingCentral Video (formerly RingCentral Meetings) is RingCentral Inc.'s dedicated video conferencing product, offering a free tier for meetings up to 100 participants and a paid Pro+ tier for larger, longer meetings with more storage and security.
enrichment.pricing_model: Freemium: a free Video Pro tier plus a paid Video Pro+ per-user monthly subscription; larger meeting capacity is also bundled into RingEX phone plans.

enrichment.seo_title: RingCentral Video Pricing 2026: Free and Pro+ Plans Compared
enrichment.seo_meta_description: See RingCentral Video's free and paid pricing, participant limits, and features for 2026, including how it compares to RingEX bundles.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: RingCentral Video is the dedicated video conferencing product from RingCentral, Inc., the publicly traded (NYSE: RNG) communications company founded in 1999 and headquartered in Belmont, California. The video product has evolved over time from what was originally branded RingCentral Meetings into today's RingCentral Video, and it is offered both as a standalone freemium product and as a bundled component of RingCentral's broader RingEX phone and messaging plans. On the free side, RingCentral Video Pro allows unlimited meetings for up to 100 participants, though each individual meeting is capped at 50 minutes, making it comparable in structure to free tiers offered by other major video conferencing vendors. For teams that need longer or larger meetings, RingCentral Video Pro+ is priced at $10 per user per month when billed annually and removes the 50-minute cap, supporting meetings of up to 24 hours in length, while also raising the participant capacity to up to 200 people. Video Pro+ additionally unlocks cloud recording for saving and sharing meetings, end-to-end encryption for more security-conscious teams, and expanded cloud storage. Beyond the standalone video product, RingCentral bundles expanded video meeting capacity directly into its RingEX unified communications plans: customers on a paid RingEX subscription automatically receive unlimited 24-hour video meetings for up to 200 participants as part of their phone plan, meaning many organizations experience RingCentral's video capabilities through their broader communications subscription rather than purchasing Video Pro+ as a separate line item. Common features across the free and paid video tiers include screen sharing, in-meeting chat, calendar integrations with tools like Google Calendar and Outlook for scheduling, and AI-powered meeting tools such as automatically generated notes, transcription, and summaries, consistent with RingCentral's broader investment in AI features across its communications portfolio. Because RingCentral Video shares its underlying infrastructure and company with RingCentral's core RingEX phone platform, its company-level facts, including the 1999 founding date, Belmont, California headquarters, and an employee count reported between roughly 4,260 and over 7,000 depending on the source, are identical to those of the parent RingCentral, Inc. business, even though the video product itself is marketed, priced, and evaluated as its own distinct offering aimed at teams that primarily need video meetings rather than a full phone system.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: RingCentral Video is the video conferencing product of RingCentral, Inc., the publicly traded communications company founded in 1999 and headquartered in Belmont, California. Formerly branded RingCentral Meetings, the product is sold both as a standalone freemium offering and bundled into RingCentral's broader RingEX phone and messaging plans, giving customers flexibility between a video-only subscription and a full communications platform.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: The free Video Pro tier supports unlimited meetings for up to 100 participants with a 50-minute per-meeting limit, along with screen sharing and chat. The paid Video Pro+ tier removes the time cap, extends meeting length to up to 24 hours, raises participant capacity to 200, and adds cloud recording, end-to-end encryption, and expanded storage, alongside AI-powered meeting notes and calendar integrations available across tiers.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: RingCentral Video Pro is free with no per-user cost, supporting meetings up to 100 participants and 50 minutes long. Video Pro+ costs $10 per user per month billed annually and expands capacity to 200 participants and 24-hour meetings. Customers on a paid RingEX phone plan also receive expanded video meeting capacity of up to 200 participants bundled into their subscription.
